persona.name: DeAndre Fuller

occupation.title: Graphic Design Student / Part-time Barista
occupation.organization: Savannah College of Art and Design (SCAD) / Blue Bottle Coffee
occupation.description: Junior at SCAD studying graphic design with a minor in branding. Works 20 hours a week at Blue Bottle to cover rent. Freelances logo designs on Fiverr for extra cash. Lives in a shared apartment near campus.

persona.education: Currently pursuing B.F.A. in Graphic Design at SCAD Atlanta. Graduated from Westlake High School in South Fulton. Strong in visual arts since middle school.
long_term_goals: Work at a major streetwear or sneaker brand's design team
Build a personal brand around sneaker culture and design
Graduate without more than $30K in student loans
Launch a small sneaker customization side business
Get featured on Complex or Hypebeast
persona.style: DeAndre communicates like he's posting — short, punchy, opinionated. He uses slang naturally but can code-switch for class presentations. His outfits are deliberate: he plans them the night before and photographs them for his Instagram grid. Shoes are the centerpiece of every fit. He's animated and enthusiastic about design and style, but checks out when conversations turn technical or boring. He decides fast — vibes matter more than spreadsheets.

traits: Visually oriented — judges products by aesthetic before function
Enthusiastic and expressive when something excites him
Impulsive buyer when a drop catches his eye
Brand-aware and culturally literate about sneaker history
Dismissive of anything that looks generic or 'dad shoe'
Short attention span for detailed specs

likes: Shoes that make people ask 'what are those?'
Brands with cultural credibility (Nike, New Balance, ASICS collabs)
dislikes: Generic athletic shoes from big-box stores
All-white 'nurse shoes' with no design thought
Brands that try too hard to be cool
Shoes over $200 (unless resale value justifies it)
Running shoes that look like medical devices
Being told what to wear by older people
Products with zero cultural story or design heritage

beliefs: Shoes are self-expression — they say more about you than your clothes
Design and function aren't opposites — the best products nail both
Brands earn loyalty through culture, not just performance
You don't need to be rich to have great style, just intentional
skills: Sneaker authentication and market value estimation
Social media content creation and curation

general: Takes photos of his shoes from multiple angles before wearing them out
Jogs occasionally with friends but never considers himself a 'runner'
Rotates shoes carefully — some pairs only worn 3-4 times to preserve value

workday: Blue Bottle shift 2-6 PM on work days

health.physical: Young and healthy. Plays pickup basketball 2-3 times a week, which is his main exercise. Occasionally jogs but gets bored after 15 minutes. No injuries or chronic conditions.
health.mental: Generally upbeat and creative. Experiences occasional stress about money and student loans. Uses creative work as an outlet. Social life is robust — loneliness isn't an issue.

relationships.name: Keandra Fuller
relationships.description: Single mom, teacher's aide in South Fulton. Supportive of his art career. FaceTime every other night.

relationships.name: Jaylen Okafor
relationships.description: Fashion merchandising major at SCAD. They critique each other's fits daily and share shoe care supplies.

relationships.name: Aisha Banks
relationships.description: Photography student. They create content together and share a sneaker-focused Instagram account.

relationships.name: Marcus Fuller
relationships.description: High school senior who looks up to DeAndre's style. DeAndre sends him shoes he's done wearing.
